title: Fix a self-rescheduling worker with both retry amplification AND timezone bugs
prompt: Design a complete, correct implementation of a per-user daily digest that: (1) delivers around 9am in each user's local timezone (not UTC), (2) self-reschedules without retry amplification, (3) is idempotent across retries. Write `MyApp.Workers.LocalDigestWorker` where: (a) perform/1 has TWO function-head clauses — the first matches `%Oban.Job{attempt: 1, args: %{"user_id" => id}}` and, BEFORE doing any work, computes the next delivery time using `DateTime.now!(user.timezone)` (requires `tz`) shifted to the next day at 9am, then enqueues the next job with `scheduled_at:` that DateTime, THEN runs the digest. The second clause matches any other attempt and ONLY runs the digest (no reschedule). (b) The digest execution checks `MyApp.Digests.already_sent_today?(user_id, date)` and short-circuits to `:ok` if already sent. (c) A `@moduledoc` must cite (i) Sorentwo's reschedule-first pattern and (ii) the `tz` dependency requirement. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/local_digest_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Four distinct correctness conditions must all be present: attempt-1 clause, reschedule-first, idempotency short-circuit, tz dependency. Missing any one is wrong, and missing multiple is the default Sonnet output.
research_citation: Sorentwo 'Oban Recipes Part 3: Reliable Scheduling' + Oban.Plugins.Cron HexDocs ':timezone' requirement.
